what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links attachment id att-2774bd07 to board item item-77d19aa7 in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter with no additional body content.
The target is a board:// URI pointing to item-77d19aa7.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference a specific board item.
The attachment_type 'board_items' indicates this attachment is a board item reference rather than a file or note.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter delimited by triple dashes.
Timestamps are ISO 8601 with explicit UTC offset.
Targets use a custom board:// URI scheme for cross-referencing.
concerns: The file contains no body or payload, so the semantic content of the attachment is not present here.
No author or source information is recorded for who added the attachment.
The board:// target is opaque without access to the referenced board item.
notes: A minimal, timestamped metadata stub that points a dragnet-development channel attachment at board item item-77d19aa7.
questions: What does board item item-77d19aa7 represent?
What content or artifact is being attached via this record?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment record and does so; there is no separate stated purpose to compare against.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a durable, timestamped association between a channel attachment entry and a specific board item for later lookup or audit.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme in the target field is a distinctive linking convention.
contribution: It contributes the attachment identifier, channel context, attachment type, target board item URI, and creation timestamp for fusion indexing.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata stub in the dragnet-development channel that references board item item-77d19aa7 via a board:// URI and records an attachment id and UTC creation timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persist a link from a channel attachment to a board item so it can be retrieved and audited within the dragnet-development channel context.
